1→---
     2→task: 11-mvp-acceptance-testing
     3→parent: h-implement-transflow-mvp
     4→branch: feature/transflow-mvp-completion
     5→status: completed
     6→created: 2025-01-09
     7→completed: 2025-01-09
     8→modules: [acceptance, mvp, validation, testing]
     9→---
    10→
    11→# MVP Acceptance Testing & Final Validation
    12→
    13→## Problem/Goal
    14→Conduct comprehensive acceptance testing against all MVP criteria defined in @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md to ensure complete implementation and production readiness. This is the final validation step before declaring Transflow MVP complete.
    15→
    16→## Success Criteria
    17→
    18→### RED Phase (Acceptance Test Framework)
    19→- [x] Write failing acceptance tests for each MVP success criterion
    20→- [x] Write failing tests for all documented user workflows and examples
    21→- [x] Write failing tests for production-scale scenarios and edge cases
    22→- [x] Create comprehensive test scenarios matching MVP specification
    23→- [x] Document any gaps between implementation and MVP requirements
    24→
    25→### GREEN Phase (MVP Criteria Validation)
    26→- [ ] All MVP acceptance criteria pass validation testing
    27→- [ ] Complete Java 11→17 migration workflow validated end-to-end
    28→- [ ] Self-healing system demonstrates successful error recovery
    29→- [ ] Knowledge base learning shows measurable improvement over time
    30→- [ ] Model registry provides complete CRUD operations
    31→- [ ] GitLab MR creation works with real repositories
    32→- [ ] All documented features work as specified
    33→
    34→### REFACTOR Phase (Production Acceptance)
    35→- [ ] All acceptance tests pass on VPS production environment
    36→- [ ] Performance meets or exceeds specified benchmarks
    37→- [ ] Multi-user concurrent usage validated
    38→- [ ] Long-term stability demonstrated (reduced duration for practical testing)
    39→- [ ] Complete acceptance sign-off against MVP requirements
    40→- [ ] Production deployment readiness confirmed
    41→
    42→## TDD Implementation Plan
    43→
    44→### 1. RED: Comprehensive Acceptance Test Framework
    45→```go
    46→// tests/acceptance/mvp_acceptance_test.go
    47→func TestMVPAcceptance_CompleteJavaTransformation(t *testing.T) {
    48→    // Should fail initially - full MVP acceptance not validated
    49→    
    50→    if testing.Short() {
    51→        t.Skip("skipping MVP acceptance tests in short mode")  
    52→    }
    53→    
    54→    env := acceptance.SetupMVPEnvironment(t)
    55→    defer env.Cleanup()
    56→    
    57→    // Test Case: Complete Java 11→17 Migration as specified in MVP.md
    58→    scenario := &acceptance.Scenario{
    59→        Name: "Complete Java 11 to 17 Migration",
    60→        Description: "Validate complete transflow workflow as specified in MVP requirements",
    61→        
    62→        // Use official MVP test repository
    63→        Repository: "https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git",
    64→        
    65→        // Exact workflow from MVP specification
    66→        TransflowConfig: `
    67→version: v1alpha1
    68→id: java11to17
    69→target_repo: https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git
    70→base_ref: refs/heads/main
    71→target_branch: refs/heads/main
    72→lane: C
    73→build_timeout: 15m
    74→
    75→steps:
    76→  - type: recipe
    77→    engine: openrewrite
    78→    recipes:
    79→      - org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
    80→
    81→self_heal:
    82→  enabled: true
    83→  kb_learning: true
    84→  max_retries: 2
    85→`,
    86→        
    87→        // Expected outcomes from MVP specification
    88→        ExpectedResults: acceptance.ExpectedResults{
    89→            Success: true,
    90→            WorkflowBranch: "workflow/java11to17/*",
    91→            BuildSuccess: true,
    92→            MRCreated: true,
    93→            MRLabels: []string{"ploy", "tfl"},
    94→            MaxDuration: 10 * time.Minute,
    95→        },
    96→        
    97→        // MVP validation steps
    98→        ValidationSteps: []acceptance.ValidationStep{
    99→            {Name: "git_clone", Description: "Repository should be cloned successfully"},
   100→            {Name: "workflow_branch", Description: "Workflow branch should be created"},
   101→            {Name: "recipe_execution", Description: "OpenRewrite recipe should execute"},
   102→            {Name: "build_validation", Description: "Build should pass via /v1/apps/:app/builds"},
   103→            {Name: "mr_creation", Description: "GitLab MR should be created"},
   104→            {Name: "mr_labels", Description: "MR should have correct labels"},
   105→            {Name: "cleanup", Description: "Resources should be cleaned up"},
   106→        },
   107→    }
   108→    
   109→    result, err := env.ExecuteScenario(context.Background(), scenario)
   110→    assert.NoError(t, err, "MVP acceptance scenario should complete without errors")
   111→    
   112→    // Validate against MVP success criteria
   113→    validateMVPCriteria(t, result, scenario.ExpectedResults)
   114→    
   115→    // Additional MVP-specific validations
   116→    assert.True(t, result.BuildVersion != "", "Should generate build version")
   117→    assert.True(t, strings.Contains(result.MRDescription, "Java 11"), "MR should describe Java 11 migration")
   118→    assert.True(t, result.ArtifactsGenerated, "Should generate build artifacts")
   119→    
   120→    // Validate build API integration (MVP requirement)
   121→    build, err := env.BuildClient.GetBuild(result.BuildVersion)
   122→    assert.NoError(t, err, "Should retrieve build details")
   123→    assert.Equal(t, "C", build.Lane, "Should correctly detect Lane C")
   124→    assert.Equal(t, "success", build.Status, "Build should be successful")
   125→}
   126→
   127→func TestMVPAcceptance_SelfHealingWorkflow(t *testing.T) {
   128→    // Should fail initially - self-healing not fully validated against MVP
   129→    
   130→    env := acceptance.SetupMVPEnvironment(t)
   131→    defer env.Cleanup()
   132→    
   133→    // Test Case: Self-healing with all three healing strategies from MVP
   134→    scenario := &acceptance.Scenario{
   135→        Name: "Self-Healing with LangGraph Integration",
   136→        Description: "Validate all MVP self-healing capabilities",
   137→        
   138→        // Repository with known compilation issues 
   139→        Repository: "https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-test-healing-scenario.git",
   140→        
   141→        TransflowConfig: `
   142→version: v1alpha1
   143→id: self-healing-test
   144→target_repo: https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-test-healing-scenario.git
   145→base_ref: refs/heads/main
   146→target_branch: refs/heads/main
   147→
   148→steps:
   149→  - type: recipe
   150→    engine: openrewrite
   151→    recipes:
   152→      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses
   153→      
   154→self_heal:
   155→  enabled: true
   156→  kb_learning: true
   157→  max_retries: 3
   158→  cooldown: 30s
   159→`,
   160→        
   161→        ExpectedResults: acceptance.ExpectedResults{
   162→            InitialBuildFailure: true,  // Expect initial failure to trigger healing
   163→            HealingTriggered: true,
   164→            HealingSuccess: true,
   165→            FinalSuccess: true,
   166→            KBLearning: true,
   167→        },
   168→    }
   169→    
   170→    result, err := env.ExecuteScenario(context.Background(), scenario)
   171→    assert.NoError(t, err)
   172→    
   173→    // Validate MVP healing requirements
   174→    assert.False(t, result.InitialBuildSuccess, "Should fail initially to trigger healing")
   175→    assert.True(t, result.HealingAttempted, "Should attempt healing")
   176→    assert.True(t, len(result.HealingOptions) >= 2, "Should generate multiple healing options")
   177→    
   178→    // Validate healing options match MVP specification
   179→    expectedOptions := []string{"human-step", "llm-exec", "orw-gen"}
   180→    for _, expected := range expectedOptions {
   181→        found := false
   182→        for _, option := range result.HealingOptions {
   183→            if option.Type == expected {
   184→                found = true
   185→                break
   186→            }
   187→        }
   188→        assert.True(t, found, "Should include %s healing option", expected)
   189→    }
   190→    
   191→    // Validate parallel execution with first-success-wins
   192→    assert.True(t, result.ParallelExecution, "Healing should use parallel execution")
   193→    assert.True(t, result.WinningStrategy != "", "Should identify winning healing strategy")
   194→    assert.True(t, result.CancelledStrategies > 0, "Should cancel non-winning strategies")
   195→    
   196→    // Validate KB learning occurred
   197→    assert.True(t, result.KBLearningRecorded, "Should record healing attempt in KB")
   198→    
   199→    // Validate final success
   200→    assert.True(t, result.FinalBuildSuccess, "Should succeed after healing")
   201→    assert.True(t, result.MRCreated, "Should create MR after successful healing")
   202→}
   203→
   204→func TestMVPAcceptance_KnowledgeBaseLearning(t *testing.T) {
   205→    // Should fail initially - KB learning not validated against MVP
   206→    
   207→    env := acceptance.SetupMVPEnvironment(t)
   208→    defer env.Cleanup()
   209→    
   210→    // Test Case: KB learning and improvement over multiple runs
   211→    testScenarios := []string{
   212→        "java-compilation-error-missing-imports",
   213→        "java-compilation-error-missing-semicolon", 
   214→        "java-compilation-error-wrong-type",
   215→    }
   216→    
   217→    for _, errorType := range testScenarios {
   218→        t.Run(errorType, func(t *testing.T) {
   219→            // Run same error scenario 3 times to validate learning
   220→            var learningProgression []acceptance.LearningMetrics
   221→            
   222→            for attempt := 1; attempt <= 3; attempt++ {
   223→                scenario := createKBLearningScenario(errorType, attempt)
   224→                
   225→                result, err := env.ExecuteScenario(context.Background(), scenario)
   226→                assert.NoError(t, err)
   227→                
   228→                // Extract learning metrics
   229→                metrics := acceptance.LearningMetrics{
   230→                    Attempt:           attempt,
   231→                    ErrorSignature:    result.ErrorSignature,
   232→                    HealingDuration:   result.HealingDuration,
   233→                    SuccessConfidence: result.HealingConfidence,
   234→                    KBCases:          result.KBTotalCases,
   235→                }
   236→                learningProgression = append(learningProgression, metrics)
   237→                
   238→                // Validate KB storage
   239→                kbHistory, err := env.KBClient.GetErrorHistory(context.Background(), result.ErrorSignature)
   240→                assert.NoError(t, err)
   241→                assert.True(t, kbHistory.TotalCases >= attempt, 
   242→                    "KB should accumulate cases over attempts")
   243→            }
   244→            
   245→            // Validate learning progression
   246→            validateKBLearningProgression(t, learningProgression)
   247→        })
   248→    }
   249→}
   250→
   251→func TestMVPAcceptance_ModelRegistry(t *testing.T) {
   252→    // Should fail initially - model registry not validated against MVP requirements
   253→    
   254→    env := acceptance.SetupMVPEnvironment(t)
   255→    defer env.Cleanup()
   256→    
   257→    // Test Case: Complete model registry CRUD as specified in MVP
   258→    modelSpecs := []models.LLMModel{
   259→        {
   260→            ID: "gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06",
   261→            Name: "GPT-4o Mini",
   262→            Provider: "openai",
   263→            Version: "2024-08-06", 
   264→            Capabilities: []string{"code", "analysis", "planning"},
   265→            MaxTokens: 128000,
   266→            CostPerToken: 0.00015,
   267→        },
   268→        {
   269→            ID: "claude-3-haiku@20240307",
   270→            Name: "Claude 3 Haiku", 
   271→            Provider: "anthropic",
   272→            Version: "20240307",
   273→            Capabilities: []string{"code", "analysis"},
   274→            MaxTokens: 200000,
   275→            CostPerToken: 0.00025,
   276→        },
   277→    }
   278→    
   279→    for _, model := range modelSpecs {
   280→        t.Run(model.ID, func(t *testing.T) {
   281→            // Test CREATE operation
   282→            err := env.ModelRegistryClient.AddModel(context.Background(), &model)
   283→            assert.NoError(t, err, "Should create model successfully")
   284→            
   285→            // Test READ operation
   286→            retrievedModel, err := env.ModelRegistryClient.GetModel(context.Background(), model.ID)
   287→            assert.NoError(t, err, "Should retrieve model successfully")
   288→            assert.Equal(t, model.ID, retrievedModel.ID)
   289→            assert.Equal(t, model.Provider, retrievedModel.Provider)
   290→            assert.Equal(t, model.MaxTokens, retrievedModel.MaxTokens)
   291→            
   292→            // Test UPDATE operation
   293→            updatedModel := *retrievedModel
   294→            updatedModel.CostPerToken = updatedModel.CostPerToken * 1.1
   295→            
   296→            err = env.ModelRegistryClient.UpdateModel(context.Background(), &updatedModel)
   297→            assert.NoError(t, err, "Should update model successfully")
   298→            
   299→            // Verify update
   300→            finalModel, err := env.ModelRegistryClient.GetModel(context.Background(), model.ID)
   301→            assert.NoError(t, err)
   302→            assert.Equal(t, updatedModel.CostPerToken, finalModel.CostPerToken)
   303→            
   304→            // Test DELETE operation
   305→            err = env.ModelRegistryClient.DeleteModel(context.Background(), model.ID)
   306→            assert.NoError(t, err, "Should delete model successfully")
   307→            
   308→            // Verify deletion
   309→            _, err = env.ModelRegistryClient.GetModel(context.Background(), model.ID)
   310→            assert.Error(t, err, "Should not find deleted model")
   311→        })
   312→    }
   313→    
   314→    // Test LIST operation
   315→    allModels, err := env.ModelRegistryClient.ListModels(context.Background())
   316→    assert.NoError(t, err, "Should list models successfully")
   317→    
   318→    // Validate CLI integration
   319→    output, err := env.CLIRunner.Run("ploy", "models", "list")
   320→    assert.NoError(t, err, "CLI models list should work")
   321→    assert.Contains(t, output, "ID", "CLI output should show model information")
   322→}
   323→```
   324→
   325→### 2. GREEN: MVP Validation Implementation
   326→```go
   327→// tests/acceptance/mvp_validation.go
   328→func validateMVPCriteria(t *testing.T, result *acceptance.Result, expected acceptance.ExpectedResults) {
   329→    t.Helper()
   330→    
   331→    // Core MVP requirements validation
   332→    mvpChecks := []struct {
   333→        name string
   334→        check func() bool
   335→        requirement string
   336→    }{
   337→        {
   338→            name: "OpenRewrite Integration",
   339→            check: func() bool { return result.RecipeExecuted && result.TransformationApplied },
   340→            requirement: "OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration",
   341→        },
   342→        {
   343→            name: "Build Validation", 
   344→            check: func() bool { return result.BuildValidated && result.BuildAPI != "" },
   345→            requirement: "Build check via /v1/apps/:app/builds (sandbox mode, no deploy)",
   346→        },
   347→        {
   348→            name: "Git Operations",
   349→            check: func() bool { 
   350→                return result.RepoCloned && result.WorkflowBranchCreated && 
   351→                       result.ChangesCommitted && result.BranchPushed 
   352→            },
   353→            requirement: "Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)",
   354→        },
   355→        {
   356→            name: "GitLab MR Creation",
   357→            check: func() bool { return result.MRCreated && result.MRUrl != "" },
   358→            requirement: "GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration",
   359→        },
   360→        {
   361→            name: "Self-Healing System",
   362→            check: func() bool { 
   363→                return !expected.InitialBuildFailure || 
   364→                       (result.HealingAttempted && result.ParallelExecution)
   365→            },
   366→            requirement: "LangGraph healing branch types with parallel options",
   367→        },
   368→        {
   369→            name: "Knowledge Base Learning",
   370→            check: func() bool { return !expected.KBLearning || result.KBLearningRecorded },
   371→            requirement: "KB read/write for learning with case deduplication",
   372→        },
   373→        {
   374→            name: "Model Registry",
   375→            check: func() bool { return result.ModelRegistryAvailable },
   376→            requirement: "Model registry in ployman CLI with schema validation",
   377→        },
   378→    }
   379→    
   380→    for _, check := range mvpChecks {
   381→        t.Run(check.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   382→            assert.True(t, check.check(), "MVP requirement failed: %s", check.requirement)
   383→        })
   384→    }
   385→}
   386→
   387→func validateKBLearningProgression(t *testing.T, progression []acceptance.LearningMetrics) {
   388→    t.Helper()
   389→    
   390→    assert.True(t, len(progression) >= 2, "Need at least 2 attempts to measure learning")
   391→    
   392→    // Validate KB case accumulation
   393→    for i := 1; i < len(progression); i++ {
   394→        current := progression[i]
   395→        previous := progression[i-1]
   396→        
   397→        assert.True(t, current.KBCases >= previous.KBCases, 
   398→            "KB should accumulate cases over time")
   399→    }
   400→    
   401→    // Validate confidence improvement trend
   402→    if len(progression) >= 3 {
   403→        firstConf := progression[0].SuccessConfidence  
   404→        lastConf := progression[len(progression)-1].SuccessConfidence
   405→        
   406→        // Confidence should generally improve or stay high
   407→        assert.True(t, lastConf >= firstConf || lastConf >= 0.7,
   408→            "Confidence should improve or maintain high levels with learning")
   409→    }
   410→    
   411→    // Validate healing duration efficiency
   412→    durations := make([]time.Duration, len(progression))
   413→    for i, p := range progression {
   414→        durations[i] = p.HealingDuration
   415→    }
   416→    
   417→    // Later attempts should not be significantly slower (learning efficiency)
   418→    if len(durations) >= 2 {
   419→        avgEarly := (durations[0] + durations[1]) / 2
   420→        avgLate := durations[len(durations)-1]
   421→        
   422→        maxAcceptable := avgEarly + 60*time.Second // Allow 1 minute variance
   423→        assert.True(t, avgLate <= maxAcceptable, 
   424→            "Later healing attempts should not be significantly slower")
   425→    }
   426→}
   427→
   428→type MVPEnvironment struct {
   429→    TransflowRunner      *transflow.Runner
   430→    BuildClient         *build.Client
   431→    GitLabClient        *gitlab.Client
   432→    KBClient            *kb.Client
   433→    ModelRegistryClient *models.Client
   434→    CLIRunner           *cli.Runner
   435→    cleanup             []func()
   436→}
   437→
   438→func (env *MVPEnvironment) ExecuteScenario(ctx context.Context, scenario *acceptance.Scenario) (*acceptance.Result, error) {
   439→    // Create transflow configuration file
   440→    configFile, err := env.createConfigFile(scenario.TransflowConfig)
   441→    if err != nil {
   442→        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create config file: %w", err)
   443→    }
   444→    defer os.Remove(configFile)
   445→    
   446→    // Execute transflow via CLI (most realistic test)
   447→    start := time.Now()
   448→    output, err := env.CLIRunner.Run("ploy", "transflow", "run", "-f", configFile, "--verbose")
   449→    duration := time.Since(start)
   450→    
   451→    // Parse results from output and API queries
   452→    result := &acceptance.Result{
   453→        ScenarioName: scenario.Name,
   454→        Duration:     duration,
   455→        Success:      err == nil,
   456→        CLIOutput:    output,
   457→    }
   458→    
   459→    if err != nil {
   460→        result.Error = err.Error()
   461→    }
   462→    
   463→    // Extract detailed results from CLI output and service APIs
   464→    env.parseDetailedResults(result, output)
   465→    
   466→    return result, nil
   467→}
   468→```
   469→
   470→### 3. REFACTOR: Production Acceptance on VPS
   471→```bash
   472→# scripts/run-mvp-acceptance-vps.sh  
   473→#!/bin/bash
   474→set -e
   475→
   476→TARGET_HOST=${TARGET_HOST:-45.12.75.241}
   477→echo "Running MVP acceptance tests on VPS: $TARGET_HOST"
   478→
   479→# Deploy latest code to VPS
   480→echo "Deploying latest transflow implementation..."
   481→./bin/ployman api deploy --monitor
   482→
   483→# Wait for deployment to stabilize
   484→sleep 30
   485→
   486→# Run comprehensive MVP acceptance tests on VPS
   487→echo "Executing MVP acceptance test suite on VPS..."
   488→ssh root@$TARGET_HOST 'su - ploy -c "
   489→    cd /opt/ploy
   490→    
   491→    # Set test environment variables
   492→    export GITLAB_URL=https://gitlab.com  
   493→    export GITLAB_TOKEN=$GITLAB_TOKEN
   494→    export PLOY_TEST_VPS=true
   495→    export PLOY_ACCEPTANCE_MODE=true
   496→    
   497→    echo \"Running MVP acceptance tests...\"
   498→    go test -v ./tests/acceptance/ -run TestMVPAcceptance -timeout 60m
   499→    
   500→    echo \"Running long-term stability test...\"  
   501→    go test -v ./tests/acceptance/ -run TestMVPStability -timeout 300m
   502→    
   503→    echo \"Running production scale test...\"
   504→    go test -v ./tests/acceptance/ -run TestMVPProductionScale -timeout 120m
   505→"'
   506→
   507→# Generate acceptance report
   508→echo "Generating MVP acceptance report..."
   509→ssh root@$TARGET_HOST 'su - ploy -c "
   510→    cd /opt/ploy
   511→    go run ./cmd/acceptance-report/main.go --output /tmp/mvp-acceptance-report.html
   512→"'
   513→
   514→# Download acceptance report
   515→scp root@$TARGET_HOST:/tmp/mvp-acceptance-report.html ./mvp-acceptance-report.html
   516→
   517→echo "MVP acceptance testing complete!"
   518→echo "Report available: ./mvp-acceptance-report.html"
   519→```
   520→
   521→## Acceptance Test Scenarios
   522→
   523→### Core MVP Scenarios
   524→1. **Complete Java Migration**: Full Java 11→17 transformation with OpenRewrite
   525→2. **Self-Healing Success**: Build failure → healing → recovery → MR creation
   526→3. **KB Learning Cycle**: Multiple runs showing knowledge accumulation
   527→4. **Model Registry Operations**: Full CRUD cycle with CLI and API validation
   528→5. **GitLab Integration**: MR creation with proper labels and descriptions
   529→
   530→### Production Readiness Scenarios
   531→1. **Concurrent Operations**: Multiple transflows running simultaneously
   532→2. **Long-Term Stability**: 4+ hour continuous operation without failures
   533→3. **Resource Efficiency**: Memory and CPU usage within acceptable bounds
   534→4. **Error Recovery**: Service failures and graceful degradation
   535→5. **Performance Validation**: Meets all documented performance targets
   536→
   537→### Integration Scenarios
   538→1. **Service Dependencies**: Consul, Nomad, SeaweedFS, GitLab integration
   539→2. **Build System**: Lane detection, artifact generation, sandbox validation
   540→3. **Storage Operations**: KB persistence, model registry storage
   541→4. **Authentication**: GitLab token handling and permission validation
   542→
   543→## Context Files
   544→- @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md - Complete MVP requirements specification
   545→- @docs/transflow/README.md - User-facing documentation to validate
   546→- @tests/fixtures/applications/ - Test repositories for validation scenarios
   547→- @CLAUDE.md - TDD framework and acceptance criteria
   548→
   549→## User Notes
   550→
   551→**MVP Acceptance Criteria Validation:**
   552→
   553→All items from @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md ✅ **Fully Implemented** section must pass:
   554→- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
   555→- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
   556→- YAML configuration parsing and validation  
   557→- Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)
   558→- Diff validation and application utilities
   559→- GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration
   560→- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`) with full end-to-end workflow
   561→- Test mode infrastructure with mock implementations for CI/testing
   562→- LangGraph healing branch types (human-step, llm-exec, orw-gen)
   563→- Fanout orchestration with first-success-wins parallel execution
   564→- Production job submission via orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()
   565→- Comprehensive test coverage for all branch types
   566→- LangGraph planner/reducer job integration
   567→- Self-healing workflow coordination
   568→- Model registry CRUD operations in `ployman` CLI
   569→
   570→**Test Execution Commands:**
   571→```bash
   572→# Local MVP acceptance testing
   573→make test-mvp-acceptance
   574→
   575→# VPS MVP acceptance testing  
   576→TARGET_HOST=45.12.75.241 make test-mvp-acceptance-vps
   577→

Based on my investigation of the task files, here's the current status of the h-implement-transflow-mvp task:

## Current Status: Transflow MVP Task

**Task Status**: ✅ **COMPLETED** but with some outstanding validation work

### What Has Been Completed:
The main h-implement-transflow-mvp task shows as **completed** with comprehensive implementation including:

- ✅ **Core MVP Implementation**: Full transflow CLI with OpenRewrite integration
- ✅ **Knowledge Base (KB) System**: Complete implementation with SeaweedFS storage
- ✅ **Self-Healing System**: All 3 branch types (human-step, llm-exec, orw-gen) implemented
- ✅ **Production Integration**: VPS deployment with HashiCorp stack (Consul, Nomad, SeaweedFS)
- ✅ **Test Coverage**: Comprehensive unit tests following TDD methodology
- ✅ **Documentation**: Updated FEATURES.md, CHANGELOG.md, and technical documentation

### What Still Needs Completion:

Based on your mention of "full migration 11->17 and KB testing haven't been done yet," I can see from the final work log (11-mvp-acceptance-testing.md) that there are indeed remaining validation tasks:

#### 1. **GREEN Phase - MVP Criteria Validation** ⏳ PENDING:
- Complete Java 11→17 migration workflow end-to-end validation
- Self-healing system validation with all LangGraph healing strategies
- Knowledge base learning progression validation over multiple attempts
- Model registry CRUD operations validation
- GitLab MR creation and lifecycle validation

#### 2. **REFACTOR Phase - Production Acceptance** ⏳ PENDING:
- VPS production environment acceptance testing
- Long-term stability validation (4+ hour continuous operation)
- Multi-user concurrent usage validation
- Production-scale performance benchmarking

### Key Issues Identified:

From the final work log (line 648-653), there are compilation issues with the acceptance tests:
- Tests use incorrect import paths and non-existent types
- Type mismatches between test expectations and actual implementation
- Tests need refactoring to align with real transflow API surface

### Files and Locations:

**Main Task File**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/sessions/tasks/done/h-implement-transflow-mvp.md`

**Acceptance Testing Work**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/sessions/tasks/done/h-implement-transflow-mvp/11-mvp-acceptance-testing.md`

**Current Branch**: `feature/transflow-mvp-completion`

### Next Steps Required:

1. **Fix Acceptance Test Compilation**: Update tests to match actual transflow implementation
2. **Execute Java 11→17 Migration Testing**: Run complete end-to-end validation 
3. **Validate KB Learning**: Test knowledge base accumulation and learning progression
4. **VPS Production Validation**: Run acceptance tests on production VPS environment
5. **Final Sign-off**: Complete MVP acceptance against all criteria

The core implementation appears to be complete, but the validation and testing phases (GREEN/REFACTOR in TDD terms) still need to be finished to fully close out the MVP.

content.content:
1→---
     2→task: h-implement-transflow-mvp
     3→branch: feature/implement-transflow-mvp
     4→status: completed
     5→created: 2025-09-04
     6→modules: [internal/cli/transflow, api/arf, internal/cli/common, cmd/ploy]
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Implement Transflow MVP - Stream 1/Phase 1: OpenRewrite + Build Check
    10→
    11→## Problem/Goal
    12→Implement the first phase of the Transflow MVP system that applies OpenRewrite recipes to a repository and performs build verification without deployment. This delivers a functional CLI that can transform Java code using proven OpenRewrite recipes and validate the changes build successfully.
    13→
    14→## Success Criteria
    15→- [x] CLI command `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml` works end-to-end
    16→- [x] YAML configuration parsing with required fields (id, target_repo, base_ref, steps)
    17→- [x] Git operations: clone repo, create workflow branch, commit changes, push branch
    18→- [x] Recipe execution integration with existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline
    19→- [x] Build check integration using SharedPush with timeout support
    20→- [x] App naming convention: `tfw-<id>-<timestamp>` for build checks
    21→- [x] Branch naming convention: `workflow/<id>/<timestamp>`
    22→- [x] Error handling and logging for each step
    23→- [x] TDD compliance: RED/GREEN/REFACTOR cycle with unit tests
    24→- [x] Integration with existing build infrastructure (internal/build/trigger.go)
    25→- [x] Timeout configuration support (default 10m for builds)
    26→- [x] Lane override support from YAML configuration
    27→
    28→## Context Manifest
    29→
    30→### How This Currently Works: ARF Recipe Execution and Build System
    31→
    32→When a user wants to apply OpenRewrite recipes, the system currently works through a well-established pipeline starting with the `ploy arf` command. The ARF (Automated Refactoring Framework) system handles recipe discovery, execution, and result capture through several interconnected components.
    33→
    34→The entry point is `internal/cli/arf/recipes.go` which provides commands like `list`, `search`, `show`, and `transform`. When executing recipes, the system communicates with the ARF controller at a configurable URL (defaulting to the platform's API endpoint). The recipe execution involves HTTP requests to endpoints like `/v1/arf/recipes` for listing and `/v1/arf/recipes/search` for finding specific recipes by query.
    35→
    36→The transformation process leverages `services/openrewrite-jvm` which is a containerized OpenRewrite engine. When recipes are applied, the system uses Git operations from `api/arf/git_operations.go` to clone repositories, create branches, capture diffs, and commit changes. The GitOperations struct provides methods like `CloneRepository()`, `CreateBranchAndCheckout()`, `CommitChanges()`, and `PushBranch()` with full error handling and context support.
    37→
    38→For build verification, the system uses `internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush()` function which creates tar archives of source code and POSTs them to the controller's `/v1/apps/:app/builds` endpoint. The build system in `internal/build/trigger.go` handles lane detection (A-G lanes for different deployment targets), artifact generation (Unikraft, OSv, OCI containers, etc.), signing, SBOM generation, and storage upload. Importantly, the build system performs comprehensive validation including policy enforcement, vulnerability scanning, and artifact integrity verification.
    39→
    40→The build process is sophisticated - it automatically detects the appropriate lane based on project structure, builds the appropriate artifact type (unikernel, VM image, container, etc.), signs the artifact, generates Software Bill of Materials (SBOM), and uploads everything to storage with verification. The system supports timeout configuration through the `DeployConfig.Timeout` field and can honor build timeouts specified in the configuration.
    41→
    42→### For New Feature Implementation: Transflow Integration Points
    43→
    44→Since we're implementing the Transflow MVP, it will need to integrate with the existing system at several key points:
    45→
    46→The CLI integration will extend `cmd/ploy/main.go` which already includes an import for `internal/cli/transflow` and a case handler. We need to implement the `transflow.TransflowCmd()` function that follows the established pattern of other CLI commands, accepting arguments and the controller URL.
    47→
    48→The YAML parsing will require a new configuration structure in `internal/cli/transflow/config.go` that includes fields like `id`, `target_repo`, `base_ref`, `target_branch`, optional `lane` override, `build_timeout` duration, and a `steps` array. The parsing should follow the pattern established in other CLI modules with proper validation and error handling.
    49→
    50→For Git operations, we'll extend the existing `api/arf/git_operations.go` rather than creating new Git utilities. The GitOperations struct already has most of what we need, but we may need to add convenience methods for the specific branch naming convention `workflow/<id>/<timestamp>`.
